title: Trace backend authentication flow
summary: Shows how a protected request moves from auth middleware into token validation and route access checks.
question: How does authentication work in this backend project?

flow.summary: Request -> auth middleware -> auth service -> route guard
flow.entrypoint: HTTP request to a protected route
flow.outcome: Only authenticated requests with the right role reach the handler.

followUps.label: Inspect auth tests
followUps.description: Open the middleware tests to confirm the success path and failure branches.

steps.fileRationale: This file is the first protected-route touchpoint where authentication begins.

subranges.label: Middleware entry
subranges.summary: Reads the incoming Authorization header and extracts the bearer token.

subranges.label: Route guard context
subranges.summary: Later route guards consume the authenticated user set up by the middleware.

explanation.title: Start in the auth middleware
explanation.what: The middleware reads the bearer token from the incoming request.
explanation.why: Every protected route enters the auth flow through this touchpoint.
explanation.how: The authorization header is parsed and its token is forwarded to the downstream validation service.
explanation.impact: Requests without a usable token fail before route handlers execute.

links.why: The extracted token is validated by the auth service before the request can continue.

branches.label: Missing token
branches.condition: The Authorization header is missing or malformed.
branches.outcome: The request fails before the route handler runs.

branches.label: Success path
branches.outcome: The request continues into token validation.

steps.fileRationale: This file turns the raw token into the verified actor used by guards.

subranges.label: Token validation
subranges.summary: Verifies the token and resolves the authenticated user.

explanation.title: Validate and decode the token
explanation.what: The auth service verifies the token and extracts the user identity.
explanation.why: Downstream authorization decisions depend on a trusted user context.
explanation.how: The service calls the token verifier, decodes claims, and returns the resolved actor for the request.
